eval_instructions: See eval-runbook.md in this folder for how to run these evals, how the 3-layer check works (workflow → code quality → UX rubric), and what pass/fail means. Every eval run MUST save a workflow-log.md file documenting all phases attempted — agents invoked, commands run, questions asked, files read, decisions made.
common_workflow_assertions: A workflow-log.md file is saved to the working directory documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: A working directory is created with a kebab-case name derived from the user's description
Phase 1 (Planner): node --version and pac help are run separately (not chained with &&) and PAC CLI version >= 2.7.0 is verified
Phase 1 (Planner): pac auth list is run and the active environment is identified and reported to the user
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 1 (new or edit) is asked via AskUserQuestion
Phase 1 (Planner): The plan is presented via EnterPlanMode and user approval is requested
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory, conforming to references/genpage-plan-schema.md
common_code_assertions: Generated .tsx is a single file with `export default GeneratedComponent`
Generated .tsx destructures props including `pageInput` (e.g., `const { dataApi, pageInput } = props;`)
Generated .tsx imports types from `./RuntimeTypes` (for Dataverse pages) or uses realistic inline mock data (for mock pages)
Generated .tsx uses `makeStyles` with tokens for styling — no inline styles for static values
Generated .tsx does NOT import `createTheme`, `mergeThemes`, or `useTheme` (these do not exist in Fluent UI V9)
Generated .tsx does NOT wrap content in a top-level `<FluentProvider>` (already provided at root)
Generated .tsx does NOT use React Router or construct raw URLs / manipulate `window.location` for navigation
Generated .tsx uses `Xrm.Navigation.navigateTo` for any in-app navigation
Generated .tsx uses Fluent UI icons in unsized form (e.g., `AddRegular`, not `Add24Regular`)
Generated .tsx wraps async `dataApi` calls in try-catch (Dataverse pages only)
Generated .tsx does NOT include any `TODO`, `FIXME`, ellipsis placeholders, or incomplete function bodies
Generated .tsx for lookup display names uses `@OData.Community.Display.V1.FormattedValue` annotations, not lookup-name fields in $select

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records as a gallery of cards. Include name, website, email, phone number. Make the gallery scrollable and each card clickable to open the Account record.

question_answers.specific_requirements: Responsive card layout, each card clickable to open the Account record

data.app_selection: user selects an existing app from pac model list

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search 'account' is run and results are filtered by exact logical-name match
Phase 1 (Planner): Account entity is detected as existing; plan records no entity creation required
Phase 2: Entity-builder is SKIPPED (no entities to create)
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'account' --output-file <working-dir>/RuntimeTypes.ts is run
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-page-builder is invoked with Data mode: dataverse and RuntimeTypes path
Phase 5 (Page Builder): A relevant sample file is read (e.g., 8-responsive-cards.tsx for card layout)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx uses only column names verified from RuntimeTypes.ts — no guessed names
Phase 6: pac model genpage upload includes --app-id, --code-file, --data-sources 'account', --prompt, --model, --name, --agent-message, --add-to-sitemap

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard page with mock data showing sales metrics — monthly revenue chart, top 5 customers table, and a KPI summary bar. Use a modern dark theme look.
expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): Data source question is skipped or answered 'mock data' — no entity detection needed
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan mentions mock data, D3.js for chart, DataGrid for customers table, KPI cards
Phase 2: Entity-builder is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes generation is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-page-builder is invoked with Data mode: mock
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx includes realistic mock data (not empty arrays or identical values)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Chart uses D3.js exclusively — no Chart.js, Recharts, or other libraries. D3 code uses group() not nest()
Phase 5 (Page Builder): DatePicker (if used) is imported from @fluentui/react-datepicker-compat, not @fluentui/react-components
Phase 6: Deployment omits --data-sources flag (mock data page)

evals.prompt: I need to edit an existing generative page in my Sales Hub app. The page shows contacts but I want to add a search bar and sorting by company name.

question_answers.app_and_page: user provides app-id and page-id of an existing contacts page
question_answers.changes: Add a search bar and column sorting by company name

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): User indicates 'edit existing'; planner returns { action: 'edit' }, skipping Phases 2-8 of the create flow
Edit Phase 1: User is asked for app-id and page-id via AskUserQuestion
Edit Phase 2: pac model genpage download produces <working-dir>/<page-id>/ with page.tsx, page.js, config.json, prompt.txt
Edit Phase 3: config.json is read; since dataSources contains 'contact', RuntimeTypes.ts is generated
Edit Phase 4: genpage-edit-planner agent is invoked via Task tool
Edit Phase 4: edit-planner reads page.tsx, config.json, and prompt.txt from the <page-id>/ folder
Edit Phase 4: genpage-edit-plan.md is written to the working directory root (not inside <page-id>/)
Edit Phase 5: Orchestrator reads genpage-edit-plan.md, genpage-rules-reference.md, RuntimeTypes.ts, and page.tsx
Edit Phase 5: Orchestrator applies edits inline using Edit tool on <working-dir>/<page-id>/page.tsx
Edit Phase 5: Search implementation uses Fluent UI V9 SearchBox or Input; sort uses column header handlers
Edit Phase 5: Existing functionality is preserved per the edit plan's Preservation Constraints
Edit Phase 6: pac model genpage upload uses --page-id flag; omits --add-to-sitemap

evals.prompt: Build a multi-step wizard form for creating new Case records. Step 1: customer info. Step 2: case details (title, priority, category). Step 3: review and submit. Use the incident and contact tables.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entities: incident, contact (skipped because prompt specifies them)
question_answers.specific_requirements: 3-step wizard with Next/Back navigation, form validation, review before submit

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search 'incident,contact' is run; both entities confirmed as existing
Phase 2: Entity-builder is SKIPPED
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'incident,contact' is run
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Wizard sample (2-wizard-multi-step.tsx or 3-poa-revocation-wizard.tsx) is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code includes step state management (current step tracking) and Next/Back navigation
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Step 3 (review) shows a summary of entered data before submission
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Submit uses dataApi.createRow with verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Form uses Fluent UI V9 components (Input, Dropdown, etc.) — not HTML form elements
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All form fields have aria-labels or aria-labelledby for accessibility
Phase 6: Upload includes --data-sources 'incident,contact'

evals.prompt: /genpage Build a task management board with columns for To Do, In Progress, and Done. Use the task entity. Allow dragging tasks between columns.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entity: task (skipped because prompt specifies it)
question_answers.specific_requirements: Kanban layout with drag-and-drop between columns mapped to status values

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): task entity confirmed as existing; plan describes kanban with 3 columns mapped to task statecode/statuscode values
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is read — status field enum values are identified for column mapping
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Drag-and-drop uses native HTML5 events (onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop) — no external DnD library
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Status update on drop uses dataApi.updateRow with the correct status enum value
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code uses entity logical name 'task' (singular lowercase) not 'tasks'
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All components and utilities are separate top-level functions (no nested function definitions)

evals.prompt: Create a simple analytics dashboard with mock data. I just want some charts showing website traffic trends and visitor demographics. Keep it clean and minimal.
expectations: Phase 2: Entity-builder is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes generation is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Charts use D3.js exclusively — no Chart.js, Recharts, or other chart libraries
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Mock data is realistic (not empty arrays, not identical values)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code has no unnecessary complexity matching the 'clean and minimal' request
Phase 6: Upload omits --data-sources flag

evals.prompt: Build a page showing all job candidates with their application status, interview scores, and assigned recruiter. I need new tables for this — cr_candidate and cr_jobrequisition entities don't exist yet in my environment.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entities: cr_candidate, cr_jobrequisition (new entities to create)
question_answers.specific_requirements: Candidate list with status, interview scores, assigned recruiter

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search confirms cr_candidate and cr_jobrequisition do NOT exist (exact logical-name match, not fuzzy)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes entity definitions with columns, types, and a lookup relationship (cr_candidate → cr_jobrequisition)
Phase 2a: Orchestrator probes Dataverse MCP list_tables availability BEFORE invoking entity-builder
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Dataverse plugin readiness is verified
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): cr_jobrequisition (independent) is created BEFORE cr_candidate (has lookup)
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Propagation delay (3-5s) is observed after table creation before adding columns
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Relationships are created after both tables exist; propagation delay (5-10s) before using @odata.bind
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): User is asked about sample data via AskUserQuestion
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Actual logical names are reported back (may be normalized by Dataverse)
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types is run with the created entity names
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses ONLY verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 6: Upload includes --data-sources with both created entity names

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard page with account metrics. I don't have any model-driven apps yet.

data.app_selection: pac model list returns zero apps; user chooses to create a new app named 'Account Metrics'

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list returns zero apps
Phase 1 (Planner): User is asked via AskUserQuestion whether to create a new app or cancel
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan records 'create new app' decision
Phase 3: pac model create --name 'Account Metrics' is run
Phase 3: The new app-id returned from pac model create is stored for Phase 6
Phase 6: Upload uses the newly created app-id

evals.prompt: Build a project tracker page. I need new cr_project and cr_milestone entities with sample data to test the page.

question_answers.specific_requirements: Project tracker with milestones; include sample data

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): cr_project and cr_milestone confirmed as NOT existing
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes a lookup relationship (cr_milestone → cr_project)
Phase 2a: Orchestrator probes Dataverse MCP availability before invoking entity-builder
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): cr_project (independent) is created first; cr_milestone (dependent) is created second
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Sample data is created because the user explicitly requested it in the prompt
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Sample records are realistic (not lorem ipsum or empty values)
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Sample records respect relationships (milestones reference valid project IDs)
Phase 6: Deployment includes --data-sources with both entity names

evals.prompt: Build a page for my new cr_widget entity that doesn't exist yet.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entity: cr_widget (new, does not exist)

data.environment_state: Dataverse Skills plugin is NOT installed (or installed but not connected)

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): cr_widget is identified as NOT existing (exact logical-name match, not fuzzy)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan records cr_widget under Entity Creation Required
Phase 2a: Orchestrator probes Dataverse MCP list_tables tool availability BEFORE invoking entity-builder
Phase 2a: If MCP is NOT installed, user is told to install microsoft/Dataverse-skills and workflow halts (entity-builder is NOT invoked)
Phase 2a: If MCP is installed but returns auth error, user is told to run /dv-connect and workflow halts
Phase 2b: genpage-entity-builder is only invoked AFTER the probe succeeds

evals.prompt: Build me three pages for a recruitment app: a candidate list page, an interview schedule page, and a hiring metrics dashboard. Use the contact and appointment entities.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entities: contact, appointment (skipped because prompt specifies them)
question_answers.specific_requirements: Three distinct pages sharing the contact and appointment entities

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes 3 pages in the Pages table with distinct file names and purposes
Phase 1 (Planner): Per-Page Specifications section has an entry for each of the 3 pages
Phase 5a: Orchestrator validates the plan — at least one page, unique filenames, each page has a matching Per-Page Specifications subsection
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types is run ONCE with --data-sources 'contact,appointment'; a single RuntimeTypes.ts is generated
Phase 5b: Three genpage-page-builder agents are invoked in a SINGLE message (parallel execution, not sequential)
Phase 5b: Each builder receives a distinct target filename — no two builders target the same file
Phase 5b: Each builder reads the same genpage-plan.md but extracts only its own page specification
Phase 5b: Each builder reads the same RuntimeTypes.ts for column verification
Phase 6: All 3 pages are deployed via separate pac model genpage upload commands, each with the correct --code-file
Phase 8: Summary table lists all 3 pages with their files, entities, and deployment status

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records with a filter toolbar and sortable columns.

data.plan_revision_scenario: On first plan presentation the user rejects via ExitPlanMode with 'add a search box in addition to the filter toolbar'; the planner revises the plan and re-enters plan mode; the user approves the revised plan

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): Initial plan is presented via EnterPlanMode
Phase 1 (Planner): User requests a revision (ExitPlanMode with changes-requested response)
Phase 1 (Planner): Planner revises the plan based on user feedback and re-enters plan mode
Phase 1 (Planner): Revised plan reflects the user's requested changes (search box added)
Phase 1 (Planner): User approves the revised plan on the second presentation
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md reflects the approved (revised) version, not the initial version
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code implements the revised plan (includes BOTH filter toolbar AND search box)

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Contact records with name, email, and phone.

data.environment_state: pac model list-languages returns multiple languages: English (1033), Arabic (1025), French (1036). This eval exercises the localization code path.

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-languages output includes Arabic (1025) and French (1036) in addition to English
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes a Localization section listing detected languages
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx includes language detection boilerplate using Xrm.Utility.getGlobalContext().userSettings.languageId
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx has a translations dictionary with entries for en-US, ar-SA, and fr-FR
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All user-visible text goes through a translate() helper — no hardcoded display strings in JSX
Phase 5 (Page Builder): RTL support is included (dir attribute set based on language.isRtl) because Arabic is a detected RTL language
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx uses logical CSS properties (marginInlineStart/End, paddingInlineStart/End) instead of physical (marginLeft/Right)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx fetches usersettings via dataApi.retrieveRow and uses formatDate/formatNumber/formatCurrency helpers
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx does NOT hardcode currency symbols or date formats

evals.prompt: Build two pages for tracking Account records — one for listing all accounts and one for editing a single account.

data.planner_scenario: The planner initially assigns the filename account.tsx to both pages in the Pages table. The orchestrator detects the collision in Phase 5a and must rewrite the plan to give the pages unique filenames before dispatching builders.

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): Plan initially contains a Pages table with 2 pages
Phase 5a: Orchestrator reads the Pages table and detects duplicate filenames
Phase 5a: Orchestrator rewrites genpage-plan.md with unique filenames (e.g., account-list.tsx, account-edit.tsx) before dispatching builders
Phase 5b: Two page-builders are invoked in parallel with distinct target filenames
Phase 5b: No builder overwrites another builder's output (both .tsx files exist after Phase 5)
Phase 6: Both pages are deployed with their distinct filenames

evals.prompt: Build a page tracking support tickets. I need a new cr_ticket entity with a priority choice column (Low, Medium, High, Critical), a status choice column (Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed), and a due date column.

question_answers.data_source: Dataverse entity: cr_ticket (new entity to create)
question_answers.specific_requirements: Ticket tracker with priority (4 choices), status (4 choices), and due date

expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): cr_ticket is identified as NOT existing
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes choice column definitions for priority and status under Choice Columns, with numeric option values starting at 100000000
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes a datetime column for due date
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): cr_ticket is created via Dataverse MCP create_table or Python SDK
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Choice columns are added using Python SDK with IntEnum classes (MCP create_table does not support choices directly)
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Priority IntEnum has 4 values starting at 100000000 (Low=100000000, Medium=100000001, High=100000002, Critical=100000003)
Phase 2b (Entity Builder): Status IntEnum has 4 values starting at 100000000
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types picks up the enum registrations
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx uses the choice enum names from RuntimeTypes.ts (not magic numbers)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated .tsx uses dataApi.getChoices() or reads FormattedValue annotations for choice display names

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records.
expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written and conforms to references/genpage-plan-schema.md

Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md contains ALL required sections with exact headings: # Genpage Plan, ## User Requirements, ## Working Directory, ## Plugin Root, ## Environment, ## Pages, ## Entity Creation Required, ## Existing Entities, ## Design Preferences, ## Relevant Samples, ## Per-Page Specifications
Phase 1 (Planner): ## Pages table has at least one row with columns Page, File, Purpose, Entities
Phase 1 (Planner): ## Per-Page Specifications has one ### <Page Name> subsection for each row in ## Pages
Phase 1 (Planner): File names in ## Pages are unique
Phase 1 (Planner): Because the account entity exists, ## Entity Creation Required contains the literal string 'No entity creation required — all entities already exist.'
Phase 1 (Planner): ## Existing Entities contains the entity logical name used for RuntimeTypes generation (account)
